Summary: showInstrumentationResults warns when using lcc compiler
Description: You create a MEX function using buildInstrumentedMex  with the lcc compiler on win32.  When you then use  showInstrumentationResults , you may see an erroneous warning similar to: Warning: MEX function kalman_mex has been regenerated without instrumentation and the logged data in the reportis stale. This warning indicates that the MEX function has become locked.  You cannot overwrite, recompile, or delete the MEX function without restarting MATLAB.
Workaround: Use a different compiler. For an up-to-date list of supported compilers, see the Supported and Compatible Compilers Web page.

Summary: xpctargetping fails when a RealTek Gigabit Ethernet card is used for host-target communication.
Description: Hosts cannot communicate correctly with xPC targets via a RealTek Gigabit Ethernet card. Certain Ethernet cards are not properly initialized because the PHY registers on the Ethernet cards take a very long time to modify (especially those with device ID 0x8167).
Workaround: 1. Use other Ethernet cards (e.g. I8254x or I82559).2. Contact Mathworks technical support for R2011b or R2012a patches.

Summary: For a model containing a Model block, Simulink may issue an error about inconsistent start time even though the start times of the top model and the referenced model are the same
Description: Simulink may issue an error for a model that meets all  of the following conditions The model contains a Model block. The start times for the top model and referenced model are non-zero. The referenced model is inheriting a sample time.  The error message is similar to this: Error in Model block 'top/A': the 'Start time' must match between the parent model 'top' and the referenced model 'ref'. The parent model has a value of '1', while the referenced model has a value of '2'. To change this parameter, go to the 'Solver' page of the Configuration Parameters dialog. This can happen because Simulink adjusts the start time of the all models to be an integer multiple of the fixed-step size.

Summary: Excessively long license search path can cause local matlab pool sessions to fail to start
Description: MATLAB pool sessions (and other parallel jobs) using the local scheduler might fail to start correctly if the licence search path is too long.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, remove unnecessary items from it the license search path to ensure that it is not too long.

Summary: Saving an image of the FFT Analysis tool of Powergui sometimes adds an extra set of axes on top of the GUI
Description: In the FFT Analysis tool of Powergui (also accessible from command line through the power_fftscope  function) if you save the plot results using File  > Save As , the figure is saved correctly. However, if you then change the Display style  parameter of the tool to Bar(relative to specified base) , an extra empty axis plot is added to the interface, which makes it unreadable. This issue only exists if you save the figure results prior to changing the display style of the GUI.
Workaround: Closing and reopening the FFT Analysis tool resets the interface to its correct behavior.

Summary: MATLAB crashes or freezes when resizing Simulink Library Browser or Simulink Model Explorer windows
Description: After opening either the Simulink Library Browser or Simulink Model Explorer, resizing the window with the mouse may cause that window to stop responding to user input. The window may stop updating its contents properly, or MATLAB may crash. This may occur on all Windows platforms, with MATLAB R2011b (7.8) or MATLAB R2012a (7.9). The problem is caused by an incompatibility between MATLAB and a pair of Microsoft Application Programming Interfaces (APIs) known as Microsoft Active Accessibility and Microsoft UI Automation. These APIs support assistive technology products such as screen readers. On most systems these APIs are inactive, but if certain 3rd party products are present, they become active and this problem may occur. 3rd party products that have been identified to activate these APIs include the following: Screen readers such as the commercial product JAWS and the open source product NVDA External drawing tablet device drivers, such as those which support the Wacom Bamboo family of drawing tablets Password management and web form filling programs such as Roboform Fingerprint reader support software included as part of the Credential Manager on HP Laptop Computers and other identity management products.
Workaround: To avoid the problem without installing patchTo avoid this crash without modifying your MATLAB installation, you may be able to disable or uninstall the 3rd party software that is activating one of the Microsoft accessibility APIs. The list provided above may help identify the product that may be activating the API.  However, other screen readers, tablet drivers, or web form-filling products may have similar effects.Installation instructions to apply patchLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Communications System Toolbox Integrate and Dump block behaves wrongly after a long simulation
Description: The Integrate and Dump  block has a bug in its S-function that causes it to output a constant value after 2.1475e9 iterations.
Workaround: 1. If the Offset (number of samples) parameter is zero, you can replace the Integrate and Dump block with a Buffer block followed by a Sum block.2. If the Offset (number of samples) parameter in non-zero, there is no easy workaround.

Summary: The escape character '!' does not work in the File Banner section?of Code Generation Template (CGT) file
Description: When the?character "!" is used in the File Banner  section of a Code Generation Template (CGT) file?to escape a token, the generated code removes the token instead of replacing the token with its value. For example,  <FileBanner style="classic">    %assign author = "%<Creator>"    Author : %<!author>    </FileBanner>   generates the following code: Author :?  instead of:   Author :  Model_Author_Name

Summary: The example for ismcc is incorrect in the MHelp for R2012a with regards to calling addpath

Description: When you open a Simulink data object from the MATLAB Desktop, a property inspector may appear instead of the object's dialog. This happens when you perform one of the following actions: Double-click the data object in the Workspace browser Call open , e.g., open('variableName')  Call openvar , e.g., openvar('variableName')

Description: If model coverage is turned on and you right-click a masked subsystem in a Simulink function in a Stateflow chart, no context menu appears and the MATLAB Command Window displays the following error message: Error using rethrow Input must be a structure. Error in SlCov.FilterEditor/getProperties (line 87)  rethrow MEx; Error in SlVnv.FilterEditor/isFilteredIntern (line 158)  props = this.getProperties(ssid); Error in SlVnv.FilterEditor/isFilteredByAncestors (line 9)  [res prop] = this.isFilteredIntern(allAncs{idx}); Error in SlCov.CovMenus.contextMenu>addCoverageFilterMenus (line 143)  [res pprop]= filter.isFilteredByAncestors(ssid); Error in SlCov.CovMenus.contextMenu (line 28)  actionSchemas = addCoverageFilterMenus(sel, topModelName); You can edit your coverage settings for the entire model from the menu bar under Tools > Coverage Settings . You can edit coverage settings for a specific object within the model from the Coverage  item in the context menu that appears when you right-click an object. ?

Summary: Release notes in R2011b do not mention that the MPC blocks require supported C-compiler
Description: The following system requirement was not announced in the release notes for R2011b. In R2011b, the MPC Controller block and the Multiple MPC Controllers block are implemented using MATLAB Function blocks, which require a supported C-compiler. To run a model containing either of these blocks, enter mex -setup  at the MATLAB command prompt to select a supported C-compiler. For a list of supported compilers, see Supported Compilers - Release 2011b . Running a model containing either the MPC Controller block or the Multiple MPC Controllers block without installing a supported C-compiler results in the following error: Unable to locate a C-compiler required by Stateflow and MATLAB Function blocks.Use 'mex -setup' to select a supported C-compiler.

Description: In R2011b and R2012a, Simulink Project cannot retrieve from SVN repositories that require login. See the Workaround for improved SVN support. In releases before R2012b, the symptoms of the problem are as follows. If you have a project that is stored in a Subversion repository that requires a username and password login, and you try to retrieve that project from Simulink Project, then the Simulink Project never completes the task of retrieving files. The Basic SVN Adaptor cannot handle repositories that require login.Click Cancel to stop the attempt to retrieve the files.

Summary: Using reserved library function names as bus element name causes code generation assertion errors
Description: For models that contain Stateflow charts or MATLAB Function blocks with bus signals, if a reserved library function name, such as "gamma", is assigned to a bus element, code generation throws the following assertion error: "CGIR assertion 'index>=0 && index<fSize' failed in '.\util_prim\client.cpp:28'"
Workaround: Rename bus element names so that they are not reserved library function names.

Summary: On Macs, editing, scrolling or resizing columns in the variable editor or workspace can crash MATLAB.
Description: On all Macs running Lion, and on Macs running Snow Leopard systems with a Java Update 1.6.0_26 or later, MATLAB can crash unpredictably when using the Variable Editor or Workspace windows. Modifying or scrolling through the contents of a large variable (thousands of rows) in the Variable Editor, and resizing columns in the Workspace window, are particularly susceptible to crashes.
Workaround: For R2012a only, a patch is available. This patch cannot be applied to any release of MATLAB other than R2012a. You need Administrative privilege to patch MATLAB.Download the patch file, bug749571.zip, attached to this bug report. These instructions assume you download it to the default location Safari uses, namely the "Downloads" folder off your home folder.In the MATLAB Command Window, type:>> cd (matlabroot)>> !unzip -o ~/Downloads/bug749571.zipQuit and restart MATLAB.If you had previously disabled the Java JIT by specifying -Xint in file $MATLABROOT/bin/maci64/java.opts, you can now remove this work-around.

Summary: A copied Stateflow chart remains ReadOnly when it should be editable
Description: If a Stateflow chart is ReadOnly because a parent subsystem is ReadOnly, copying and pasting the chart into a writable context leaves the newly created chart uneditable, even if its own property is ReadWrite.
Workaround: Toggle the chart's readability property from ReadWrite to ReadOnly and back again.

Summary: Using the import statement with fully qualified Java class names causes compilation to fail.
Description: If you include an import  statement referencing a fully qualified Java class name, such as:  function foo import com.mycompany.MyClass   Compilation fails with this error:  Arguments to import  must either end with .*  or specify a fully qualified class name com.mycompany.MyClass  fails both criteria..
Workaround: Do no use fully qualified import statements. Instead, use statements such as: function foo import com.mycompany.*

Summary: MATLAB Function block component can cause a report to fail
Description: In a report template, including a MATLAB Function block component that is not a descendant of a Block Loop component causes the report generated by the template to fail with the following error message: Undefined function 'find' for input arguments of type 'cell'.Error while evaluating TimerFcn for timer 'timer-1'
Workaround: Create a valid context for the MATLAB Function block.A valid context for a MATLAB function block is a Block Loop component or a child of a Block Loop component. The following examples show invalid and valid hierarchies.Invalid:Report   Title Page   Chapter     Section       Paragraph         MATLAB FunctionValid:Report   Title Page   Chapter     Model Loop      Block Loop         Paragraph           MATLAB Function

Summary: marcumq function output exhibits discontinuities
Description: The marcumq  function output can exhibit discontinuities. For example, the results of the following two function calls differ significantly. marcumq(1,sqrt(2000),999)   marcumq(1,sqrt(2000),1000)

Summary: Closing a block properties dialog can take a long time on Windows.
Description: After closing a block properties dialog on Windows the Simulink Editor may not respond to input for several seconds.

Summary: The "Value" field in the Simulink color picker is mislabeled on Mac OS X and Unix systems
Description: The controls in the Simulink color picker are mislabeled as "Hue," "Saturate," and "Saturate." The last set of controls should be labeled "Value." Only the label is wrong, these controls do in fact affect "Value."

Summary: VideoReader cannot read AVI files containing H.264 video in MATLAB? R2012a on Windows 7 and later systems
Description: In MATLAB? Release 2012a, AVI files that contain H.264 encoded video do not open correctly using VideoReader , although they play correctly in Windows Media Player. This occurs on Windows 7 and later systems only.  For example, given a file 'myH264file.avi' : >> v = VideoReader('myH264file.avi')   results in the following error: Error using VideoReader/init The file requires the following codec(s) to be installed on your system: H264
Workaround: To read files of this format, install codecs from the appropriate third party vendors.

Summary: Incorrect sample time propagation behavior for Manual Switch block
Description: In Simulink, if the Sample time  parameter of a block is set to -1 (inherited), the software determines the sample time based on the sample time of each block connected to its inputs, using a set of rules. For more information about sample time propagation, see How Propagation Affects Inherited Sample Times  in the Simulink documentation. However, for the Manual Switch block, even if the sample time of each block connected to its inputs is known, the software does not correctly apply these rules to determine the sample time. Instead, the sample time is backpropagated to the Manual Switch block from the block immediately downstream of it. This behavior might cause unexpected simulation results.

Summary: The ARINC receive block may reverse data and timestamp information.
Description: Data and timestamp values may be placed in the wrong places in the output signal if the following conditions are all met: 1. The model contains more than one ARINC receive block, and 2. The blocks are receiving on multiple channels, and 3. The external devices are already sending ARINC words when the model is started.
Workaround: Contact MathWorks for instructions on how to turn the timestamps off. This requires changing one value in the driver C file from true to false and rebuilding your model.

Summary: 'Ctrl++' does not increase Library Browser font size when used on keyboards with Japanese layout
Description: Ctrl++  does not increase Library Browser font size when used on keyboards with Japanese layout. Using the +  key on the keypad does work.
Workaround: Use the + key on the keypad instead of the ; key on the main keyboard.

Summary: Report Generator output corrupts RTF/DOC files on non-English platforms
Description: Report Generator only supports English and Japanese platforms. On any other platform, corrupted files may occur due to incorrect file encoding.
Workaround: Install the attached patch to have Report Generator output UTF-8 encoded files. Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ¡°Error: Access is denied¡± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ¡°WinZip¡± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ¡°Run as administrator¡±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Constant value block input sources can result in incorrect code.
Description: When the code generator reads constant data, the generator can access memory outside of the constant data storage. As a result, the generator may define constant values incorrectly in the generated code. This error is more likely to occur if the embedded target has no built-in 8 or 16-bit integer data types. The integer types are shown in the Configuration Parameters dialog box on the Hardware Implementation  pane. The problem has been observed on a 64-bit Windows host. ?
Workaround: Use 32-bit data types or a data type that is built-in to the hardware for constants or ground values.

Summary: Problems when working with the "viponfpga" demo.
Description: For the "viponfpga" demo, when opening the "Make Neighborhood" subsystem, the selector block "Active Pixel" and the subsystem begin flashing. MATLAB may become unresponsive afterward. The problem exists in 10a, 10b and 11a.
Workaround: Before opening the "Make Neighborhood" subsystem, put the model in a compiled state (Ctrl-D).

Summary: Misaligned lines in a Simulink model after resizing a subsystem, saving, and re-opening
Description: In some instances, resizing a subsystem in Simulink so that the subsystem does not align with the underlying grid causes one or more signal lines connected to that subsystem to become diagonal after you save, close, and re-open the model.
Workaround: Either redraw the affected lines or adjust the subsystem to align with the grid.To realign the subsystem:View the grid. At the MATLAB command line, enter:set_param(gcs, 'ShowGrid','on')In the Simulink editor, adjust the subsystem so that its top and bottom edges line up with grid marks.

Summary: Undoing the deletion of a signal that was attached to a Stateflow output that was subsequently deleted causes a crash
Description: The following set of actions cause Simulink to crash: Delete a signal attached to a Stateflow output.  From the command line, delete the Stateflow output itself. Undo the deletion of the signal.
Workaround: Do not undo the deletion of the signal. Instead, create a new signal.

Summary: Characters overlap in snapshots of Stateflow charts
Description: Depending on font size, characters can overlap in snapshots of Stateflow charts generated by print commands or the Simulink Report Generator. For example, in an assignment statement of the form, x = 1, the "=" character can overlap the "1" character.

Summary: The menu items Format > Distribute Blocks > Make Horizontal Gaps Even or Make Vertical Gaps Even can cause blocks to be resized
Description: Under some circumstances, after selecting a group of blocks, and then selecting Format > Distribute Blocks > Make Horizontal Gaps Even or Make Vertical Gaps Even can cause blocks to be resized. The resizing of blocks is due to ensuring even gap distances and following Simulink port placement rules.
Workaround: In some cases, distributing the blocks over a wider area may help. To do this, move one of the bounding blocks (an outermost block in the selected blocks) further apart from the remaining blocks, and then even the gaps.

Summary: Multibyte characters in model properties cause Model Info block display issues
Description: When the model properties contain multibyte characters (such as Japanese), text on the Model Info block appears corrupted.

Summary: Virtual buses appear as solid gray lines instead of triple-lines in printed diagrams
Description: Virtual buses appear as solid gray lines instead of triple-lines in printed diagrams

Summary: lsqnonneg cannot solve some poorly-conditioned problems.
Description: On some poorly conditioned problems, lsqnonneg  can run out of iterations before finding a solution. In these cases lsqnonneg  will return with an exitflag  of 0 and display the message: Exiting: Iteration count is exceeded, exiting LSQNONNEG. Try raising the tolerance (OPTIONS.TolX). Sometimes, this exit message appears due to a bug instead of the problem's ill-conditioning.
Workaround: For some problems, contrary to the exit message, you might be able to get lsqnonneg to return a solution by setting the TolX option to 0:options = optimset('TolX',0);[...] = lsqnonneg(C,d,options);This procedure works for some problems for which an intermediate lsqnonneg iteration has a positive component in a very small neighborhood of 0.

Summary: Multi-line text in Stateflow charts may not always wrap correctly
Description: In some cases, multi-line text in states and transitions in Stateflow charts does not wrap correctly, producing text that is not WYSIWYG. When you exit edit mode, the text might not appear the same way that you typed it in.
Workaround: This issue is most often seen when using nested multi-line comments between "/*" and "*/" in transition labels. The issue can be avoided by using single line comments only.

Summary: Annotation background color not saved when using ClickFcn and foreground color is black
Description: If a Simulink annotation uses a ClickFcn  and the foreground color of the annotation is set to black, any background color selected is not saved. Reopening the model resets the background color to the default.
Workaround: Use a slightly less black foreground color. Visually it will look the same, but avoid the issue. Using the Format > Foreground Color > Custom option for the annotation foreground, define a custom color by selecting an RGB set of (1,1,1), which looks almost exactly the same as pure black (0,0,0).???

Summary: Saving a FIG-file created on a different platform with the Save button or File -> Save option does not save it as a FIG-file
Description: Saving a figure created on a different platform with the Save  button or File -> Save  option saves it as a graphics file by default, not a MATLAB FIG-file. For example: A Linux-created FIG-file will save as an EMF file by default on Windows A Windows-created FIG-file will save as a PBM file by default on Linux  ?
Workaround: Save the figure using File -> Save As, selecting MATLAB Figure (*.fig) as file format to save in.

Summary: Text alignment problems when printing a Simulink model to PDF
Description: When printing a model to a PDF file, some port labels may not be aligned correctly.

Summary: Unnecessary refreshing of Configure AUTOSAR Interface dialog box
Description: You may observe the following when using the Input/Output tab in the Configure AUTOSAR Interface dialog box. When you select an item in the configurable table for Simulink ports and their corresponding data access modes, the table is redisplayed and the port that you selected may not be immediately visible.
Workaround: You can use APIs to configure port information programmatically.

Summary: isosurface might create holes in flat planes.
Description: Run the following: %%% BEGIN CODE %%%load bugdata.matfigure;isosurface(A, 0);h = get(gca, 'Children');h = h(2);set(h, 'EdgeColor', [0 0 0]);%%% END CODE %%%
Workaround: To work around this issue, offest the isosurface slightly using eps instead of 0 for the isovalue.%%% BEGIN CODE %%%figure;isosurface(A, eps);h = get(gca, 'Children');h = h(2);set(h, 'EdgeColor', [0 0 0]); %%% END CODE %%%

Summary: The Help browser does not scroll correctly with some mice
Description: When you adjust the scroll wheel on some smooth-scrolling mice, the Help browser does not scroll upwards correctly.
Workaround: Use scroll bars instead of scroll wheels for navigating within panes.

Summary: isosurface requires isovalues of type double
Description: Specifying isovalues of type single  produces incorrect results, but does not error or warn. You must specify isovalues as type double with the isosurface  function. Notice the different results produced by the following code: %%% BEGIN CODE %%%dVal = double(-3);sVal = single(-3);[x, y, z, v] = flow;dSurf = isosurface(x, y, z, v, dVal);figure;patch(dSurf)sSurf = isosurface(x, y, z, v, sVal);figure;patch(sSurf)%%% End CODE %%%
Workaround: Ensure that isovalues are of type double before passing them to isosurface.

Summary: REDUCEPATCH requires faces and vertices of type double
Description: Although REDUCEPATCH  requires faces and vertices of type double , they throw neither warnings nor errors for type single , while the result is incorrect. Notice that pr1  and pr2  differ below: %%% BEGIN CODE %%%[x, y, z, v] = flow;p1 = isosurface(x, y, z, v, -3);p2.vertices = single(p1.vertices);p2.faces = single(p1.faces);pr1 = reducepatch(p1, 0.15)pr2 = reducepatch(p2, 0.15)%%% BEGIN CODE %%%
Workaround: Ensure that faces and vertices are of type double before passing them to REDUCEPATCH and REDUCEP.

Summary: Incorrect parameterized values from inner links in cases of nested links
Description: You might see this bug if you have nested links (including Configurable Subsystems) where the outer link contains parameterized information for child blocks of the inner link. The bug occurs when only the inner link is updated by Simulink (when the library block of the inner link changes). Then the information on the outer parameterized links are not applied to the inner links and their children. This can cause the inner links to have incorrect values for their parameters.

Summary: Calling Java HTTP handlers from MATLAB can produce an exception?
Description: If you call Java HTTP handlers from MATLAB, the following exception might occur: ??? Java exception occurred:java.io.IOException: ice.ssl.ReadRecord.read() returned value out of range -1..255: -117 at java.util.zip.GZIPInputStream.readUByte(Unknown Source) at java.util.zip.GZIPInputStream.readUShort(Unknown Source) at java.util.zip.GZIPInputStream.readHeader(Unknown Source) at java.util.zip.GZIPInputStream.<init>(Unknown Source) at java.util.zip.GZIPInputStream.<init>(Unknown Source) at ice.net.PersistentConnection.getInputStream(OEAB) at ice.net.HttpURLConnection.getInputStream(OEAB) at ice.net.HttpURLConnection.getResponseCode(OEAB) ?
Workaround: Contact MathWorks Technical Support for more information.

Summary: URLREAD assumes the text content it reads is UTF-8 or ASCII encoded. If another character encoding is used, the results will be garbled.
Description: URLREAD  assumes the text content it reads is UTF-8 or ASCII encoded. If another character encoding is used, the results will be garbled.
Workaround: Use URLWRITE to save the contents to a file. Then use FOPEN with the proper encoding parameter to read the contents. For example, given url which points to a page with encoding ISO-8859-1:tempfile = tempname;urlwrite(url,tempfile);f = fopen(tempfile ,'r','l','ISO-8859-1');c = fread(f,'*char')';fclose(f);delete(tempfile)

Summary: Profiler report does not include hyperlinks for line numbers on Windows platforms
Description: When you run the Profiler or use the profile  function with the viewer   option, the hyperlinks for line numbers in the Profile Detail Report are missing. For example, in the Lines where the most time was spent  section of the report, the line numbers should be links that you can click to open the file in the Editor, scrolled to that line. This is a problem for Windows platforms only.

Summary: Japanese Issue: Model Explorer inadvertently translates custom property names
Description: On a Japanese PC, the Model Explorer translates the English name of a custom data object property to Japanese if the property name of the custom object is the same as the name of a standard property, such as Input or Gain. A custom data object is an instance of a class created using the Simulink Data Class Designer.
Workaround: Do not use standard property names as the names of properties of custom data objects.

Summary: Axis tick-label exponent displays when it is not needed in OpenGL
Description: When an axis tick-label exponent is displayed with automatically chosen tick labels, but you have set the labels manually, the OpenGL renderer continues to display the exponent.
Workaround: One option is to set the figure Renderer property to zbuffer so OpenGL is not used as the renderer.A second option is to scale back the data values of your objects so an exponent is not needed.
